risks.title: First-party surfaces regress to REST loopback
risks.summary: Web UI, TUI, CLI, scheduler, or daemon-local flows could call /api/** internally and bypass the intended service/control seams.
mitigations: Architecture rules explicitly ban API-as-internal-bus behavior.

risks.title: External API lacks mature auth/TLS basis for internal mutation trust
risks.summary: Localhost assumptions or API-key-like headers are not enough to make API mutation paths safe for internal first-party control.
mitigations: Use websocket/control and local services for first-party flows.
Reconsider only after real auth and SSL/TLS support.

risks.title: Business logic leaks into connectors
risks.summary: Connector-local policy, routing, guard, session, model, scheduler, or tool logic creates parity drift and duplicated execution paths.
mitigations: Move shared behavior behind pipeline or connector-agnostic service seams.

risks.title: Turn fails after thinking with no user-visible explanation
risks.summary: Provider failures, retries, guard exhaustion, or deadline failures can end a turn without a useful operator-facing message.

risks.title: Malformed historical tool-call spans poison fallback requests
risks.summary: Assistant messages with unpaired tool_calls can cause provider 400s and cascade across retries/fallbacks.
mitigations: Sanitize malformed tool-call history at LLM request boundary.
Test all providers with malformed history cases.
Keep final model route separate from failed attempts.

risks.title: Cloud provider funding or quota exhaustion
risks.summary: Cloud model routes can fail due to exhausted credits, quota, auth, or provider outages.

risks.title: Local model resource contention distorts quality and latency
risks.summary: CPU/GPU/disk contention can increase timeouts and materially lower benchmark or soak quality for local models.
mitigations: Separate model latency from framework latency.
Skip same-provider models after local framework unreachable failures.

risks.title: Development tests corrupt production local state
risks.summary: Running tests, soaks, or manual drills against the same database as the production agent can corrupt or poison live user state.
mitigations: Use isolated test homes and testutil.TempStore.
Never touch production ~/.flowforge-dev during development validation.
Add release-gate evidence of isolated config root.

risks.title: embedded SQL store/FTS corruption blocks runtime subsystems
risks.summary: Memory FTS or other embedded SQL store corruption can break heartbeat, curation, memory, or startup work.
mitigations: Do not share production DB with tests.

risks.title: Local commands construct fake state instead of talking to the live daemon authority
risks.summary: CLI/TUI code could construct a separate runtime and report fake success for daemon-live state such as in-memory circuit breakers.
mitigations: Local/direct rule scoped to local durable state.
Daemon-live-only state requires non-REST daemon-control seam.
Commands fail loudly until the seam exists.

risks.title: Agency/autonomy settings increase unattended authority without confirmation
risks.summary: A UI, API, CLI, or rogue process could raise agency/autonomy, scopes, action classes, concurrency, cadence, notifications, or cloud budgets without clear operator consent.
mitigations: No ordinary API mutation path for authority-bearing changes.

risks.title: Dashboard renders stale, empty, or non-functional UI state
risks.summary: Topic refreshes, disconnected websocket state, missing snapshots, or partial controls can produce empty pages, broken clicks, or misleading fields.

risks.title: Observability labels report healthy state for unavailable providers
risks.summary: Provider status such as healthy/hot/headroom can be misleading when providers are unconfigured, inaccessible, or token exhausted.
mitigations: Source health labels from provider evidence, not absence of errors.

risks.title: Scheduled cron jobs execute late or fail to notify
risks.summary: Cron jobs can be configured and enabled but fail to run, fail to record execution, or fail to notify the operator.
mitigations: Execution history and manual run controls.

risks.title: Agent injects or drifts to unrelated topics from memory/context
risks.summary: Long turns or memory-heavy requests can drift into unrelated topics, fabricate capability constraints, or lose destination continuity.
mitigations: Memory intake and topic guard tests.

risks.title: Secrets leak through UI, logs, telemetry, or integration errors
risks.summary: Provider keys, mail credentials, channel tokens, and billing account-adjacent secrets may be exposed if snapshots or logs are not redacted.

risks.title: Skill/plugin catalog entries are incomplete or invalid
risks.summary: Catalog install can fail when source packages lack required manifests or metadata. Official hosted entries now publish explicit source metadata; live install smoke remains required after site deployment.
mitigations: Do not report successful refresh when zero useful entries are available without explanation.
Official catalog entries publish source_url and source_subdir.

risks.title: Tool/MCP surfaces drift from pipeline availability and proof rules
risks.summary: Tools may appear available, unavailable, or executed differently depending on surface if not centralized.
mitigations: Central tool availability through pipeline/service seams.

risks.title: Voice or camera capture violates consent or retention expectations
risks.summary: Audio, transcripts, images, and camera frames are highly sensitive and can be captured, retained, or routed unexpectedly.
mitigations: Named media device inventory and selection.
Canonical media provenance and retention fields.

risks.title: External integration assumptions differ from real user setups
risks.summary: Mail, provider, channel, and local bridge configurations vary across users and operating systems.
mitigations: Live manual smoke against representative configurations.

risks.title: Framework soaks are polluted by incidental model changes
risks.summary: Changing model routes during behavior soaks can make it unclear whether failures belong to the framework or the model.
mitigations: Run local framework soaks against intentional local routes.
Repeat against the cloud provider for provider compatibility only after local success.

risks.title: Windows updater sidecar remains locked after upgrade
risks.summary: Windows can block removal of old updater sidecars while the daemon or another process holds the executable.
mitigations: Stop daemon and rerun upgrade as preferred next step.
Elevated PowerShell fallback only when rerun cannot remove it.

risks.title: Agent product expertise is based on stale or incomplete docs
risks.summary: If source docs or wiki pages lag behind user-facing behavior, the agent can confidently explain obsolete setup paths, missing controls, or wrong troubleshooting steps.
mitigations: Visible surface to documentation coverage checks.
Answers cite sources and expose missing-doc gaps.

risks.title: Installed daemon starts with wrong config, HOME, PATH, or service user
risks.summary: The service manager may start FlowForge with a different environment than the one the operator validated.
mitigations: ServiceInstallConfig captures config path, FLOWFORGE_* env, PATH, and HOME.

risks.title: Behavioral correction quality depends on a local embedder — now defaulted, probed, and release-gated locally, with a hard build floor still pending
risks.summary: The semantic correction layer's accuracy rides on the embedding client. Selection is now local-first: the bundled local embedder (embed-model via Embedded Runtime) is preferred over cloud providers, the daemon probes its reachability at boot, hard-warns when it falls back, and reports the honest tier (CLI /status, REST/websocket agent_status, dashboard System Health); `flowforge models provision-embedder` pulls the model on demand and `flowforge models embedder-check` is a non-zero-exit release gate. Two silent-degradation bugs were found and fixed: an /api/embed response-parser bug that left the semantic tier returning empty vectors, and a provider-merge bug where embedding fields were not backfilled so struct-serialized settings configs shadowed the embedding endpoint and resolved to the n-gram floor. The second was caught only by running `flowforge models embedder-check` / `status` against a real instance (both now report semantic); the offline and daemon soaks missed it because they bypassed config resolution / passed on lexical guards. A working embedder is still not enforced as a hard build floor (CI has no local Embedded Runtime), so a host without Embedded Runtime can still run behavioral correction on the n-gram floor — but it is now loud (boot warn + degraded tier on every surface + a local release gate) rather than silent.
mitigations: Done: local-first selection makes the bundled local embedder (embed-model via Embedded Runtime) the default over cloud providers; the daemon probes reachability at boot and falls back to the n-gram floor honestly, and `flowforge models provision-embedder` (CLI) or the pinned Settings → Embedding panel (via the `models.provision-embedder` WebSocket control — the dashboard runtime-blocks direct internal /api/ fetches) pulls the model on demand and hot-swaps the live embedder without a restart. The embedder is a single shared self-rebinding client (EmbeddingClient.Rebind swaps the provider in place via atomic.Pointer), so one reprovision activates the tier across every consumer — memory ingestion/retrieval, the pipeline behavioral guards, and plugin/tool routes; a prior gap re-pointed only the memory holders and left the guards (the actual correction embedder) on the stale n-gram client while /status reported semantic.
Done: the active embedder tier (semantic vs ngram-fallback) is surfaced in CLI /status, the REST/websocket agent_status, and the dashboard System Health card.

Done: a build-tag-gated A/B soak (src/engine/embedder_soak_test.go) measures guard paraphrase recall over a held-out corpus (semantic 0.90 vs n-gram 0.03 after tuning) and recommends per-category thresholds; it surfaced and verified the fix for the /api/embed parser bug. FALSE_COMPLETION and FINANCIAL_ACTION_CLAIM thresholds were tuned to 0.675; re-validating on a larger corpus remains worthwhile.

Done (local): `flowforge models embedder-check` is a non-zero-exit release gate (release-prep skill gate 10) and the daemon hard-warns at boot on the n-gram floor. A hard CI/build-fail floor is intentionally deferred since CI has no local embedder.

Partial (slice 6): CAPABILITY_DENIAL and FINANCIAL_ACTION_CLAIM semantic centroids are now wired into their guards (all 5 categories live, each bounded by tool-truth). Deleting the magic-string lexical fallbacks is deferred until a hard embedder floor exists, since a no-Embedded Runtime host still degrades the semantic path to the n-gram floor.
Planned: include the active embedder tier in benchmark run metadata.
Planned: expunge the lexical guard fallbacks once a hard embedder floor is guaranteed.

Done: central grounding for behavioral guards (2026-05-29, found by the manual-agent-exercise). The guards fail-closed the agent on normal requests because each used a coarse ground-truth proxy: absolute cosine conflated benign smalltalk with a financial claim ('Hello there, friend!' scored 0.707 > 0.675), and 'any tool ran' was treated as 'the denied capability exists'. Fixed centrally: (a) guard semantic scores are now a baseline margin (cos to category centroid minus a neutral centroid); (b) one central, domain-agnostic completion-assertion signal gates the financial and false-completion guards so refusals/explanations/questions are not mistaken for claims; (c) capability-denial is grounded on a successful tool of the matching capability class (introspection no longer proves an action capability). Verified live end-to-end.

Planned (follow-up phase): the over-firing class spans multiple guards (financial, execution/capability-denial, task_deferral observed). Per-guard grounding gates are holding for v1.1.0 but are whack-a-mole; the systemic end-state is ONE central grounding judge (given response + tool results + tool surface, is any claim/denial contradicted by reality?), likely a small local model call gated behind a cheap pre-filter. Tracked as its own phase; escalate from gates to the judge if more guards surface.
